title: lamium gevorense
side_effects: Lamium gevorense can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to the presence of toxic compounds like pyrrolizidine alkaloids. Ingestion of the plant can also lead to liver damage, as the toxins can cause inflammation and fibrosis in the liver tissue. Additionally, improper use of lamium gevorense can cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing in sensitive individuals. In severe cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can lead to cardiovascular problems and even organ failure.
